title: Protocol for Investigating Periglomerular Afferent Innervation in Mouse Renal Cortex
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cv2vs","text":"This protocol aims to investigate the anatomical relationship between renal glomeruli and afferent axons in the mouse kidney, focusing on those identified by TRPV1 lineage and CGRP immunolabeling. The study seeks to elucidate the potential mechanosensory role of periglomerular afferent axons in monitoring glomerular pressure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"cu0ld","text":"Handle all reagents and biological materials according to institutional biosafety guidelines. Use personal protective equipment (PPE) including gloves, lab coats, and safety goggles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Anesthetize mice using isoflurane.

Perform cardiac perfusion with ice-cold calcium-free Tyrode’s solution (in mM: NaCl 116, KCl 5.4, MgCl2 1.6, MgSO4 0.4, NaH2PO4 1.4, glucose 5.6, NaHCO3 26) followed by Lana's fixative (4% paraformaldehyde and 0.2% picric acid in 0.1 M phosphate buffer pH 6.9 ).
Remove kidneys and store in PBS until sectioned.
Decapsulate kidneys and section coronally into 150 µm slices using a Vibratome. Store serial sections in PBS at 4 °C until staining.
Incubate sections in blocking buffer (PBS with 0.3% Triton-X100; 1% BSA, 1% normal donkey serum) at. 4 °C Overnight .
Incubate sections in primary antibodies diluted in blocking buffer for 48:00:00 at 4 °C .
Wash sections 3 times for 00:30:00 each in PBS at Room temperature .
Incubate in secondary antibodies for 24:00:00 at Room temperature .
Wash sections again in PBS and mount on gelatin-coated slides.
Use a Nikon A1R FLIM Confocal Microscope equipped with the A1R GaAsP Confocal system to capture images of immunolabeled sections.
Wash sections in PBS 3 times for 02:00:00 each at Room temperature .
Incubate in 50% CUBIC-L in diH2O for 24:00:00 in a 37 °C water bath.
Transfer to 100% CUBIC-L and incubate in a 37 °C water bath for 48:00:00 , refreshing the solution after 24 hours.
Perform cardiac perfusion with ice-cold calcium-free Tyrode’s solution (in mM: NaCl 116, KCl 5.4, MgCl2 1.6, MgSO4 0.4, NaH2PO4 1.4, glucose 5.6, NaHCO3 26) followed by 4% PFA in PBS.
Dehydrate slide-mounted sections with increasing concentrations of ethanol (50, 75, 100, and 100% in diH2O, 30 minutes each).
Clear slide-mounted sections in Xylenes until tissue appears transparent.
Coverslip using DPX Mountant.
Process composite images into multiple maximum intensity projections of 2-6 optical sections, taking care to ensure that individual glomeruli are not duplicated between projections.
Most slices will be imaged as 5-15 optical sections with a z-step between 5 and 15 µm.

Load each composite image into the custom MATLAB scripts that localizes immunofluorescently labeled glomeruli. Individual glomeruli will be presented for the user to score whether a fine fiber-like structure is closely apposed to the nephrin+ labeling. Scripts used: Glomerular_Depth_Characterization.m; SmartBorder.m; simpleGlomFinder.m; play_script.m
Decapsulate kidneys and section coronally into 150 µm slices using a Vibratome.
Remove kidneys and post-fix in 4% PFA for 24:00:00 . Store in PBS until decapsulation and sectioning.
Follow the CUBIC tissue clearing protocol as described by the manufacturer. These steps are briefly summarized below:
Wash slices in PBS three times for 02:00:00 each, then proceed through staining protocol.
Incubate slice in diluent at room temperature for 48 hours.
Incubate slice in primary antibody diluted appropriately in diluent at room temperature for 48 hours.
Wash slice three times in PBS for 2 hours.
Incubate in secondary antibody diluted appropriately in diluent at room temperature for 24 hours.
Wash slice three times in PBS for 2 hours.
Place slice in 50% CUBIC-R + in diH2O solution for 24 hours at room temperature.
Place slice in 100% CUBIC-R + for at least 24 hours, and store at room temperature, protected from light until ready for imaging.

Just prior to imaging, gently transfer slice to a 40mm round #1.5 glass coverslip mounted in a circular chamber and submerged in CUBIC-R + medium. Use two 0.1 mm thick, 40mm round silicone gaskets as spacers, and a second coverslip gently placed on top before screwing the well shut. Take care to ensure no bubbles remain within the medium to prevent movement during long image collection periods.

Image the cleared, immunostained tissue using a Nikon FN1 upright stand equipped with an A1R HP MP laser scanning head and a motorized Prior stage and piezo Z drive, controlled with NIS Elements 5.1 software. Select individual glomeruli whose entire volume is accessible to image at 0.16 μm/px resolution.
Estimate the distance between CGRP+ and nephrin+ labeling in CUBIC-processed slices using both 3-D and 2-D analysis methods.
Create 3-D models of the nephrin+ labeling and CGRP+ axon bundles in Imaris 9.8 using the Surfaces Wizard function.
Perform a distance transformation to determine the shortest distance between each CGRP+ signal and the nephrin+ signal.
Load images as a composite of nephrin+ and CGRP+ labeling in Fiji software, adjusting the contrast to better visualize labeling.

Advancing through the entire volume of the image, stack two adjacent optical sections at a time into a maximum intensity projection. Use the line tool in Fiji to record the closest distance between CGRP+ and nephrin+ labeling for each projection.
This method can also be used to estimate the distance between CGRP+ axons and Bowman's space.
creator: Roman Tyshynsky
